mnemonics.title: Special PIDs Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: PIDs 0 and 1 are special: Think '0' for Zero (Swapper/Scheduler - nothing to swap initially), '1' for 'One' (Init - the one and only first process). Remember: 0 is the Scheduler, 1 is the Origin of all processes.

mnemonics.title: fork() Return Values
mnemonics.content: Parent gets Positive (child's PID), Child gets Zero (0), Failure gets Negative (-1). Think 'P-Z-N': Parent-Zero-Negative.

mnemonics.title: Zombie vs Orphan
mnemonics.content: Zombie = 'Z' for Zzz... (dead but still in table waiting). Orphan = 'O' for Orphaned (no parent, needs adoption by init). Zombies need wait(), Orphans need init().

acronyms.usage: Unique numeric ID assigned to each process by the kernel
PID of the parent process, obtained via getppid()
Identifies a group of related processes for job control
Identifies a session containing multiple process groups
Kernel process responsible for swapping and CPU scheduling
First user-space process, ancestor of all other processes

visualTips.visualization: Imagine an upside-down tree: Root is PID 1 (init), branches are parent-child relationships. PID 0 is the invisible ground (kernel scheduler).
Think of a fork in the road: Parent goes one way (gets child's PID), child goes other way (gets 0). If road blocked, returns -1 (failure).
Picture a zombie: Body is gone (process terminated) but still standing in the room (process table) because someone (parent) hasn't signed the death certificate (wait()).
Imagine a child alone on street - parent is gone. Police (init, PID 1) adopts them. The child continues walking (executing) under new supervision.
Think of a library catalog: Each book (process) has a unique number (PID) in the catalog (process table). Kernel looks up process by its PID like librarian finds book by number.

analogies.analogy: Like a Social Security Number for processes - unique identifier assigned at 'birth' (creation) and used throughout its lifetime.
Like a school attendance register - records all students (processes) currently in school with their unique roll numbers (PIDs).
Like a graduated student who hasn't collected their degree certificate - technically done (terminated) but still in university records until paperwork is complete.
Like a child whose parents passed away - they need a guardian (init process) to take care of them and handle their affairs.
Like asking 'What is your name?' (getpid) and 'Who is your parent?' (getppid) - fundamental identity questions for processes.
